2603.26461.title: Neuro-Symbolic Process Anomaly Detection
2603.26461.paper_summary: Process anomaly detection is an important application of process mining for identifying deviations from the normal behavior of a process. Neural network-based methods have recently been applied to this task, learning directly from event logs without requiring a predefined process model. However, since anomaly detection is a purely statistical task, these models fail to incorporate human domain knowledge. As a result, rare but conformant traces are often misclassified as anomalies due to their low frequency, which limits the effectiveness of the detection process. Recent developments in the field of neuro-symbolic AI have introduced Logic Tensor Networks (LTN) as a means to integrate symbolic knowledge into neural networks using real-valued logic. In this work, we propose a neuro-symbolic approach that integrates domain knowledge into neural anomaly detection using LTN and Declare constraints. Using autoencoder models as a foundation, we encode Declare constraints as soft logical guiderails within the learning process to distinguish between anomalous and rare but conformant behavior. Evaluations on synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that our approach improves F1 scores even when as few as 10 conformant traces exist, and that the choice of Declare constraint and by extension human domain knowledge significantly influences performance gains.
2603.26461.paper_summary_zh: 過程異常檢測是過程挖掘的一個重要應用，用於識別過程正常行為的偏差。基於神經網絡的方法最近被應用於這項任務，直接從事件日誌中學習，而不需要預定義的過程模型。然而，由於異常檢測是一項純粹的統計任務，這些模型未能融入人類的領域知識。因此，稀有但符合的痕跡經常因其低頻率而被錯誤分類為異常，這限制了檢測過程的有效性。最近在神經符號人工智慧領域的發展引入了邏輯張量網絡（LTN），作為將符號知識整合到神經網絡中的一種手段，使用實值邏輯。在這項工作中，我們提出了一種神經符號方法，通過使用LTN和Declare約束將領域知識整合到神經異常檢測中。以自編碼器模型為基礎，我們將Declare約束編碼為學習過程中的軟邏輯引導，以區分異常行為和稀有但符合的行為。對合成和真實世界數據集的評估表明，即使存在少至10個符合的痕跡，我們的方法也能提高F1分數，並且Declare約束的選擇以及由此延伸的人類領域知識顯著影響性能提升。
2603.26461.author: Devashish Gaikwad et.al.
2603.26461.authors: Devashish Gaikwad, Wil M. P. van der Aalst, Gyunam Park
